title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: New York, NY (collaborating with Tel Aviv HQ)
description: Finout, a cloud cost-management SaaS backed by $85M+ and trusted by brands like The New York Times and Wiz, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. Requires 7+ years in RevOps, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, proven experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and a leader-doer mindset comfortable building flows, dashboards, and data models across globally distributed teams including the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, proven B2B SaaS RevOps expertise aligns perfectly with Head of Revenue Operations role, Salesforce admin + pipeline forecasting skills directly match needs, and Tel Aviv HQ collaboration leverages his geographic familiarity. Only minor gap is NY primary location vs. his preference for remote/Tel Aviv area.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations opening and immediately thought of your background—scaling RevOps across $30M+ ARR organizations in B2B SaaS is exactly what the CRO needs as Finout scales beyond its current momentum. Your Salesforce expertise and track record building analytics flows for globally distributed teams, particularly with Israeli tech hubs, positions you uniquely for this role bridging New York and Tel Aviv.

title: Revenue Operations Manager
description: Sona is hiring a Revenue Operations Manager to own and optimize its go-to-market systems and processes. The role focuses on supporting sales, marketing, and customer success operations, managing CRM architecture, building reporting and forecasting frameworks, and driving cross-functional alignment to accelerate revenue growth in a high-growth B2B SaaS environment.
score_reason: Exceptional match—Revenue Operations Manager role directly aligns with Omri's core expertise in RevOps, GTM systems, CRM architecture, and pipeline forecasting in B2B SaaS. Remote location and high-growth stage are ideal fits.
ai_opener: Hi there—I noticed Sona is building out its RevOps function, and this role resonates deeply with my 15+ years scaling GTM operations in B2B SaaS. I've spent the last decade architecting CRM systems, building forecasting frameworks, and driving cross-functional alignment that directly impact revenue acceleration—exactly what you're looking to own here.

title: Lead GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Engine, a business-travel and spend-management platform serving 20,000+ companies, is building a Growth Engineering function and seeks a Lead GTM Engineer to own and shape it. You'll build systems that aggregate and transform signals to power outbound and growth, design automations and internal tools, and partner with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Data. Requires production software experience, strong Python proficiency, familiarity with GTM systems like Salesforce and Outreach, and prior people-leadership experience or readiness for a team-lead role.
score_reason: Strong GTM and Salesforce expertise with proven leadership makes this appealing, but the role's heavy emphasis on Python production engineering and Growth Engineering (vs. RevOps operations) is outside Omri's core wheelhouse. Best fit if he's ready to pivot toward technical engineering leadership rather than pure operations.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM motions through Salesforce, pipeline forecasting, and revenue systems—but Engine's vision of building a Growth Engineering function that transforms raw signals into outbound velocity caught my attention. With your 20K+ customer base, the opportunity to architect GTM automation and internal tooling at scale aligns with where I see RevOps evolving. I'm curious whether you're open to candidates who lead through systems thinking and cross-functional partnership, even if my production Python background is fresher than ideal.

title: GTM Engineer, Revenue Operations
description: Remote seeks a GTM Engineer within Revenue Operations to design and maintain a robust GTM tech stack that streamlines outbound prospecting and minimizes manual effort. Responsibilities include implementing automated workflows, developing reporting and ROI analyses, ensuring data integrity, building automated data pipelines, and creating Ideal Customer Profiles. You'll work closely with Sales, Revenue Operations, and Technology teams and report to the Director of Revenue Systems & Processes, with a focus on AI-driven prospecting and workflow automation.
score_reason: Perfect alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise, AI-native focus, and technical stack proficiency. Remote global position matches location preferences, and the role directly leverages his pipeline forecasting, automation, and data integrity strengths.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines for B2B SaaS companies, and your focus on AI-driven prospecting workflows and automated data pipelines immediately resonated—this is exactly where RevOps is heading. I've architected similar tech stacks that reduced manual prospecting effort by 60%+ while improving pipeline predictability, and I'm energized by the opportunity to scale that approach at Remote.

title: GTM Engineer

You'll own strategy and execution of high-impact automations across the GTM stack using tools like Salesforce, Gong, Apollo, Claude, Zapier, Make, and n8n. title: GTM Engineer
description: Mento, a remote-first leadership development company doubling revenue with clients like Vercel, Airbnb, and Brex, is hiring its first GTM Engineer to build the revenue engine from scratch. Requires 3-5 years in GTM Ops, RevOps, or Growth Engineering at a B2B SaaS company, comfort with HubSpot, Apollo, Clay, SQL, APIs, and webhooks, a data-quality obsession, an AI-forward mindset with experience in agentic workflows, and a fast-shipping bias. score_reason: Excellent match on GTM Engineer role, B2B SaaS background, HubSpot expertise, and AI-native mindset. Primary concern: 15+ years experience significantly exceeds 3-5 year requirement, which may create overqualification risk or compensation misalignment, though the 'build from scratch' challenge could be compelling.

With deep HubSpot, API, and AI automation experience, I'm curious whether you're looking for someone to architect the long-term revenue infrastructure or whether the overqualification concern would be a blocker.

title: GTM Engineer

His deep expertise in pipeline forecasting and data-driven customer journey design aligns perfectly with your vision of an AI-first GTM backbone, and he's actively looking to apply RevOps architecture to next-generation AI-native platforms.

title: GTM Automation Engineer
description: Postscript, a 100% remote company backed by Greylock and Y Combinator, seeks a GTM Automation Engineer to be the technical architect of its AI-driven GTM motion. You'll systematize sales and marketing workflows, build hybrid automations blending low-code tools (Zapier, n8n, Retool) with custom Python/SQL scripts, develop internal tools using AI-assisted coding (Replit, Claude Code), and orchestrate data across Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, and Snowflake. Ideal for someone technically scrappy who deeply understands the sales/marketing engine.

title: Staff GTM Engineer (AI & Automation)
description: Grafana Labs seeks a Staff GTM Engineer (AI & Automation) to build next-generation AI agent and workflow systems. You'll spend most of your time writing code—designing multi-agent architectures, integrating LLM APIs, and building data pipelines that run 24/7 across Marketing, RevOps, and SDR teams. Requires hands-on LLM experience (prompt engineering, RAG, function calling, agent orchestration), fluency with AI-assisted development tools, automation system experience, and integration experience with Salesforce and marketing automation platforms like Customer.io, HubSpot, or Marketo.
score_reason: Strong GTM and RevOps domain expertise with Salesforce/HubSpot fluency, but this role is heavily engineer-focused (multi-agent architectures, LLM APIs, data pipelines) requiring hands-on coding skills that aren't evident in a RevOps executive profile. Better suited for a GTM engineer with deep AI/automation coding background.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building GTM engines at scale in B2B SaaS. I've architected RevOps stacks across Salesforce, HubSpot, and marketing automation platforms, and I'm increasingly focused on AI-native workflows for pipeline generation and SDR automation. Your Staff GTM Engineer role caught my attention—the multi-agent orchestration angle is exactly where RevOps is heading. I'd love to explore whether my GTM ops depth combined with growing AI automation hands-on experience could accelerate your agent systems rollout.

title: GTM Engineer
